problem: Let m_1≤\cdots≤ m_k and n be sufficiently large. If T is a tree on n vertices and G is the complete multipartite graph with vertex class sizes m_1,\ldots,m_k then prove thatR(T,G)≤ (\chi(G)-1)(R(T,K_{m_1,m_2})-1)+m_1.
original_latex: Let $m_1\leq\cdots\leq m_k$ and $n$ be sufficiently large. If $T$ is a tree on $n$ vertices and $G$ is the complete multipartite graph with vertex class sizes $m_1,\ldots,m_k$ then prove that\[R(T,G)\leq (\chi(G)-1)(R(T,K_{m_1,m_2})-1)+m_1.\]
additional_context:
Chv\'{a}tal \cite{Ch77} proved that R(T,K_m)=(m-1)(n-1)+1.

This problem is #16 in Ramsey Theory in the graphs problem collection.

References

[Ch77] Chv\'{a}tal, V., Tree-complete graph Ramsey numbers. J. Graph Theory (1977), 93.
reference_proof_hint:
Let (m_1\le \cdots \le m_k) and let
[
G=K_{m_1,m_2,\dots,m_k}
]
be the complete $k$-partite graph with vertex-class sizes (m_1,\dots,m_k). Then (\chi(G)=k), and the smallest color class in a proper $k$-coloring of $G$ has size
[
\sigma(G)=m_1.
]

Set
[
r:=R\left(T,K_{m_1,m_2}\right).
]

We will prove
[
R(T,G)\le (\chi(G)-1),(r-1)+m_1.
]

---

## Step 1: A standard “tree vs fixed graph” upper bound for large $n$

A theorem of Chvátal–Rödl–Szemerédi–Trotter (settling Burr’s conjecture for trees) says:

> For every fixed graph $H$, there exists (n_0(H)) such that for every tree $T$ on (n\ge n_0(H)) vertices,
> $ R(T,H)=(n-1)(\chi(H)-1)+\sigma(H) $
> In particular,
> $R(T,H)\le (n-1)(\chi(H)-1)+\sigma(H)$

Apply this with $H=G$. Since (\chi(G)=k) and (\sigma(G)=m_1), for $n$ sufficiently large we get
[
R(T,G)\le (n-1)(k-1)+m_1.
]
Equivalently,
[
R(T,G)\le (\chi(G)-1)(n-1)+m_1.
]

---

## Step 2: The trivial lower bound (R(T,K_{m_1,m_2})\ge n)

We claim (r\ge n). Indeed, consider (K_{n-1})
